title: SWD U13 Sevens squad bids farewell to Primary School Rugby
description: OUDTSHOORN NEWS - For the SWD U13 Sevens...
bodyText: [{"type":"paragraph","text":"OUDTSHOORN NEWS - For the SWD U13 Sevens squad, the national tournament in Bloemfontein was the final chapter in a remarkable primary school rugby journey.","html":"OUDTSHOORN NEWS - For the SWD U13 Sevens squad, the national tournament in Bloemfontein was the final chapter in a remarkable primary school rugby journey."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"This event captured the essence of what the team has come to represent: resilience, unity, and an unwavering passion for the game that has shaped them since their debut as the U12 SWD Eagles."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The tournament brought together top teams from across the region, including the Golden Lions, Pumas, Free State, Border, Eastern Province, Zimbabwe, the Valke, and SWD. From the opening whistle, the SWD boys showcased their trademark flair, proudly representing their province.","html":"The tournament brought together top teams from across the region, including the Golden Lions, Pumas, Free State, Border, Eastern Province, Zimbabwe, the Valke, and SWD. From the opening whistle, the SWD boys showcased their trademark flair, proudly representing their province."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Months of rigorous training, countless hours on the road and shared victories and defeats have forged a brotherhood among these young athletes. Their synergy on the field, evident in every pass, tackle, and try, was a testament to the trust and camaraderie built over seasons of playing together.","html":"Months of rigorous training, countless hours on the road and shared victories and defeats have forged a brotherhood among these young athletes. Their synergy on the field, evident in every pass, tackle, and try, was a testament to the trust and camaraderie built over seasons of playing together."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Despite the challenges they faced, including a long journey to Bloemfontein and the lack of proper meals upon arrival, the team delivered a series of outstanding performances.","html":"Despite the challenges they faced, including a long journey to Bloemfontein and the lack of proper meals upon arrival, the team delivered a series of outstanding performances."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"On day one, they faced a demanding schedule of four matches that resulted in three wins against EP, Border and Zimbabwe and a narrow loss against the Golden Lions.","html":"On day one, they faced a demanding schedule of four matches that resulted in three wins against EP, Border and Zimbabwe and a narrow loss against the Golden Lions."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Game 1: SWD vs Eastern Province - Victory, 31 - 10","html":"Game 1: SWD vs Eastern Province - Victory, 31 - 10"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Game 2: SWD vs Golden Lions - Narrow loss, 19 - 21","html":"Game 2: SWD vs Golden Lions - Narrow loss, 19 - 21"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Game 3: SWD vs Border - Victory, 42 - 14","html":"Game 3: SWD vs Border - Victory, 42 - 14"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Game 4: SWD vs Zimbabwe - Dominant win, 59 - 0","html":"Game 4: SWD vs Zimbabwe - Dominant win, 59 - 0"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Day two brought fresh challenges and controversy. The semi-final draw sparked debate among team managers, with concerns raised over the transparency and fairness of the match pairings. Despite the uncertainty, the SWD squad remained focused and composed, defeating the Lions 19 - 12 in a hard-fought semi-final.","html":"Day two brought fresh challenges and controversy. The semi-final draw sparked debate among team managers, with concerns raised over the transparency and fairness of the match pairings. Despite the uncertainty, the SWD squad remained focused and composed, defeating the Lions 19 - 12 in a hard-fought semi-final."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"In the final, SWD faced the Free State Cheetahs. Although they ultimately finished as runners-up, the boys gave their all, leaving their hearts on the field and earning admiration from everyone there.","html":"In the final, SWD faced the Free State Cheetahs. Although they ultimately finished as runners-up, the boys gave their all, leaving their hearts on the field and earning admiration from everyone there."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Head Coach Ralph Michaels expressed deep pride in his team, “These boys have grown through rugby, and it’s evident in their skill, discipline, respect, and character. They’ve shown what it means to play for each other and for the jersey. That spirit will carry them far beyond this week.”","html":"Head Coach Ralph Michaels expressed deep pride in his team, “These boys have grown through rugby, and it’s evident in their skill, discipline, respect, and character. They’ve shown what it means to play for each other and for the jersey. That spirit will carry them far beyond this week.”"},{"type":"blockquote","text":"For parents and supporters, the tournament was a bittersweet experience, filled with excitement, pride and a touch of nostalgia. Many have watched these players grow from their first mini-rugby matches into confident young athletes ready to take on the challenges of high school rugby."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"While the event was spirited and competitive, some questioned whether Bloemfontein remains the ideal venue for a national schools tournament of this calibre. Sparse crowds and limited media coverage left many wondering if more could be done to elevate the platform these young talents deserve.","html":"While the event was spirited and competitive, some questioned whether Bloemfontein remains the ideal venue for a national schools tournament of this calibre. Sparse crowds and limited media coverage left many wondering if more could be done to elevate the platform these young talents deserve."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"As the final whistle blew, the SWD U13 Sevens squad closed the curtain on their primary school rugby careers, with pride, memories and the promise of even greater things to come.","html":"As the final whistle blew, the SWD U13 Sevens squad closed the curtain on their primary school rugby careers, with pride, memories and the promise of even greater things to come."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’","html":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’"}]

images.alt: SWD U13 Sevens squad bids farewell to Primary School Rugby
